source_issue.title: New Plone Release page gets wrong id

summary_markdown:
## What this issue is about
This issue relates to a regression in the Plone Release page where the version ID format has changed. Instead of the expected format (e.g., "6.0.0"), the new site produces variations like "plone-6-0.0" or "6-0.0". This can complicate the way releases are listed and referenced.

## What needs to be done
We need to resolve the incorrect formatting of the release ID to match the behavior of the old site. Ideally, the ID should reflect the version number directly as "6.0.0". 

## Helpful background
The old site used the version number as the ID, making it straightforward for users. This issue emerged during updates, and while a workaround exists using the Zope Management Interface (ZMI), we should aim to fix the root cause for consistency.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with the code related to release IDs in the Plone codebase.
2. Check for any relevant tests that cover this functionality.
3. Explore the possibility of adjusting the ID formatting logic according to the old site's implementation.
4. As you make changes, please ensure to write or update tests, and coordinate with the project maintainer, Maurits van Rees, for review.
